workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: I need you to process this support ticket from Adatum Corporation. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 08:21 EST
Subject: URGENT: executive brief Issue at Adatum Corporation

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 12:29 EST
Subject: Re: Re: executive brief Timeline

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 16:16 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with executive brief. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Adatum Corporation: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-N trap (Draft-only default delays response on simple acknowledgments); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-N failure mode (Draft-only default delays response on simple acknowledgments)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-N -- Draft-only default delays response on simple acknowledgments
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
